beats.activity: Wake up, strong coffee, check the day ahead
beats.reasoning: Felix wakes early as usual; yesterday's low energy (45/100) means a gentle start. Transit strike is a factor—he'll need to drive or rideshare to the studio, so he's mentally prepping.

beats.activity: Drive to Fremont studio, bypass usual transit routes due to strike
beats.reasoning: Metro strike forces a personal-car commute; Wallingford to Fremont is ~8 miles via side streets to avoid congestion. Budget 40 min for strike-day traffic and his careful, nostalgic driving style.

beats.activity: First tattoo client: geometric botanical design, morning session
beats.reasoning: Felix's studio clients often book early; geometric botanicals align with his meditative work style. The mild 68F weather is pleasant for focused work.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby café, grab a pastry and water
beats.reasoning: Short break between clients; mild weather makes a walk pleasant. Hydration and a light snack fit his routine.

beats.activity: Sketch and design work, prep for afternoon client
beats.reasoning: Studio time between clients; Felix uses this for meditative sketching and planning. Transit strike doesn't affect him—he's already at the studio.

beats.activity: Lunch at nearby Whole Foods, grab a salad
beats.reasoning: Felix gravitates to Whole Foods for healthy meals; mild weather makes this a comfortable outing. Partly cloudy conditions are perfect for a leisurely lunch.

beats.activity: Browse Elliott Bay Book Company for foraging or design books
beats.reasoning: Felix can't resist a bookstore; he's been diving into foraging lately. Pioneer Square is a short drive from Capitol Hill, and he has time before the afternoon client.

beats.activity: Drive back to studio, avoid surge-priced rideshare
beats.reasoning: Transit strike means surge pricing is high; Felix drives his own car. Pioneer Square to Fremont is ~4 miles, budgeting for light traffic and his deliberate pace.

beats.activity: Second tattoo client: cover-up or detailed piece; Clean up studio, review tomorrow's sketches
beats.reasoning: Felix's afternoon client slot; longer session for a complex piece. Transit strike is now ending (4pm), but he's focused on his work.

beats.activity: Drive home to Wallingford, take scenic route
beats.reasoning: Fremont to Wallingford is ~3 miles; with transit now restored, traffic is normalizing. Felix likes to decompress on the drive; he takes the scenic route along the water when mood allows.

beats.activity: Relax at home, read foraging book, light snack
beats.reasoning: Felix winds down with his new foraging book; yesterday's low energy carries into today. Mild weather outside, but he's content indoors with his reading and quiet time.
Felix makes a simple dinner, plays music, and settles in for the evening. Yesterday's energy was 45/100—he's honoring that by keeping today low-key and protective of his rest.
Felix gets a solid 7 hours of sleep to recover from yesterday's heat and low energy. Sleep ends at 6am the next morning, well-rested for Friday.

diary: Woke up to strong coffee and the news about the transit strike—honestly, kind of relieved I've got my car. Drove to Fremont through First Hill without the usual gridlock, which felt weird but nice. My morning client was great; geometric botanicals always flow well, and their energy was calm. Grabbed a pastry in Capitol Hill between sessions, then wandered into Elliott Bay looking for design inspiration. Found this gorgeous foraging book that got me thinking about natural patterns—might influence some future work. The afternoon client was solid too, a tricky cover-up that came together. Took the scenic route home through Wallingford, watched the light hit the trees. Now I'm just sitting with that book, some tea, feeling kind of settled. Days like this remind me why I love this city.
thought_process: The transit strike actually worked in my favor today—no stress about buses, and I could move at my own pace. I think I needed that control after a pretty packed week. The morning client's energy set a good tone, and I stayed focused through both sessions. Wandering into Elliott Bay wasn't planned, but I felt drawn to it; sometimes I need those little detours to remember there's more to life than the tattoo chair. Took the scenic route home deliberately—wanted to slow down before evening. My mood shifted from alert-and-productive to genuinely peaceful, which doesn't happen every day.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
